mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This mechanism reflects underlying economic health and investor confidence.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value 133852.0 at t-1mo indicates strong employment
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows consistent growth, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a certain threshold, it signals potential erosion of purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to investor concerns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon as monetary policy may tighten to curb inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL value 231.025 at t-1mo is above threshold
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods when CPIAUCSL is above 230.0, or if the average return in such periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust retail sales and a low unemployment rate indicates strong consumer demand and economic capacity utilization. This synergistic effect boosts corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current values indicate strong demand and low unemployment
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both RSAFS is above 400000.0 and UNRATE is below 8.0, or if the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a monetary tightening cycle, which typically increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.1 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16 at t-1mo, potential for increases
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 0.1pp increase in FEDFUNDS over 3 months, or if the average return is not significantly negative in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts indicate strong consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which has a ripple effect across various sectors of the economy, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon due to increased economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST value 894.0 at t-2mo is above threshold
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods when HOUST is above 850.0, or if the correlation between HOUST and forward returns is not consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy. This growth supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value 97.509 at t-1mo is above threshold
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods when INDPRO is above 97.0, or if the average return in such periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market, which typically translates to strong consumer confidence and spending power. This supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one week.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE value 7.7 at t-1mo is below threshold
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 8.0, or if the average return in such periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust GDP growth and contained consumer price inflation creates a 'Goldilocks' economic scenario, where strong economic activity does not trigger aggressive monetary tightening. This environment fosters positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 13638.1 at t-5mo, CPIAUCSL 231.025 at t-1mo
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive and CPIAUCSL growth is moderate (e.g., < 0.3% MoM), or if the interaction term is not statistically significant.
